translation.en: There is heaven and earth in my heart.

notes_md:
**Trời đất** literally means “heaven and earth,” but in real life it’s mostly an emotional exclamation like “Oh my God!” or “Good grief!”

It’s a fixed pair (trời + đất) and often stands alone to show surprise, shock, frustration, or disbelief. You can stretch it for stronger emotion (trời đất ơi).  

It can also refer to the natural world or the universe in a poetic or dramatic way.  

Close in feeling to *ôi trời*, *trời ơi* — all express strong emotion, just different flavor.

content.sentence: Trời hôm nay thật đẹp, có ___.

translation.en: The weather today is beautiful, there is heaven and earth.

exampleSentencesTranslated: Heavens, I forgot to bring the keys!
When she heard that news, she could only exclaim heavens.
The heavens are so clear and cool today, I really like it.

example.es: El cielo y la tierra son hermosos hoy.
example.vi: Trời đất thật đẹp hôm nay.
example.en: The sky and earth are beautiful today.

definitions.en: The sky and the earth; often used to refer to the universe or nature.
definitions.es: El cielo y la tierra; a menudo se usa para referirse al universo o la naturaleza.
definitions.vi: Trời và đất; thường được dùng để chỉ vũ trụ hoặc thiên nhiên.

notes:
Trời đất literally means “heaven and earth,” but in real life it’s mostly an emotional exclamation like “Oh my God!” or “Good grief!”

It’s a fixed pair (trời + đất) and often stands alone to show surprise, shock, frustration, or disbelief. You can stretch it for stronger emotion (trời đất ơi).  

It can also refer to the natural world or the universe in a poetic or dramatic way.  

Close in feeling to ôi trời, trời ơi — all express strong emotion, just different flavor.
exampleSentences: Trời đất ơi, anh quên mang theo chìa khóa rồi!
Khi nghe tin đó, chị chỉ biết thốt lên trời đất.
Trời đất hôm nay thật trong lành và mát mẻ, em rất thích.
